_comment: SEED rules installed into <worktree>/doc/workstreams/rules/journey.json on first validate. These are the BASELINE machine-check rules every unit inherits (AC-5, AC-9): journey-001 = script structure guard, journey-skip = standalone-skip-gesture guard, journey-handoff = hand-off-is-a-pointer guard, journey-agent-exhausted = agent-track-exhausted-before-hand-off precondition, journey-no-stuck-escalation = a stalled harness-drivable criterion is an agent-owned blocker (two exits: fix-and-rerun or record a deferred_risk), never a human STOP fork. Per-unit corrections append/supersede here; the seed is never the whole list. The skill copies this file into the worktree if journey.json is absent, then merges any unit-specific rules already present.

rules.rule: The validate HUMAN-track script's STRUCTURE is enforced deterministically, never by an LLM re-reading the script (the gameable-judge pattern AC-9 abolishes). Before the script is handed to the human, run the structure guard against it; a non-zero exit means DETERMINISTICALLY REJECT the script and re-prompt the script-writing sub-agent with the printed reasons — never fix it inline, never let a structurally-invalid script reach the human. The guard checks: exactly one main path (more only when explicitly flagged a genuine spine fork), a hard step ceiling, a present and correctly-placed OPTIONAL error-case section after the main path, the format bans (no checkboxes, no status lines, no horizontal rules, no prose under Assert), letter+number step IDs, at least one persistence check, and an observable state change in each step's Assert. The only thing left to the human is the irreducible question the machine cannot answer: is this the right minimal architectural spine.

Skipping the validate HUMAN track ('no user surface') is honored ONLY on an EXPLICIT, STANDALONE human gesture whose ONLY subject is the skip (AC-9). It is NEVER inferred, derived, or bundled from an answer to any other question — closure surface, scope, gate choice, anything — even one that logically implies the skip. The agent may PROPOSE the skip but must ask it as its OWN question and record the verbatim question and the human's verbatim direct yes; until that direct yes about the skip is recorded the human track stays LIVE. Before treating the human track as skipped, run this check against the unit's state file; a non-zero exit means the skip is NOT ratified — keep the human track live and produce the script as normal. Never hand-wave the skip on prose memory.

The chat turn that hands the validate HUMAN-track script to the human is a POINTER, NEVER a re-narration (AC-9). It carries only: the script's location, the single thing the human must judge (is this the right minimal architectural spine), and the GO/NO-GO ask. It MUST NOT restate the script's own contents — not the steps, not the load-bearing bet's exposition, not the setup block, not the warm-up/edge caveats; those already live in the self-contained script. This is the AC-1 'point, don't dump' rule applied to the message that most tempts duplication. Before sending the hand-off turn, persist its exact text to {handoff_path} and run this check; a non-zero exit means the turn is not a pointer — rewrite it as a pointer (do NOT just trim, since length is NOT the load-bearing property — a re-narration crushed onto one short line still re-narrates), regenerate, and re-check. The check enforces the POSITIVE SHAPE a real pointer always has and rejects everything else: it REQUIRES (a) a LOCATOR — a script path/locator the human can open; (b) the JUDGMENT named — the word 'spine' (is this the right minimal architectural spine); and (c) an explicit GO/NO-GO ask. It then enforces LITTLE ELSE by rejecting the step-sequence payload that is the substance of any re-narration: more than a token number of ordered-step connectors (then/next/→/numbered chain) or per-step expected-outcome markers (expect/should/you'll see/verify/confirm/observe/appears). A hard length ceiling plus the literal step-ID (A1/B2) and Arrange/Act/Assert bans remain as SECONDARY backstops, not the primary test. All keys are DOCUMENTED structural properties of the script format or load-bearing phrases the contract itself names (go/no-go, spine) — never a magic phrase, never an LLM judging an LLM. Never hand-wave this on prose memory; the persisted turn + guard are the gate, exactly as the structure guard is the gate for the script itself.

AGENT TRACK MUST BE EXHAUSTED BEFORE HAND-OFF (AC-9) — the human never does what a harness can. A criterion belongs to the AGENT track iff the project's own harness/test tooling can drive it END-TO-END on the REAL system (real daemon, real UI render, real transport, asserting on real external state) — NOT merely an in-process call. EVERY such harness-drivable criterion must be agent-verified by that real-system harness (or explicitly deferred-as-risk) BEFORE the human script is handed over; an in-process unit pass is NEVER agent-verification of an e2e/real-daemon criterion. A criterion may reach the human ONLY if no harness can drive it to a real-system assertion (genuine human judgment — visual taste, restart-and-relive). This is a HAND-OFF PRECONDITION that runs BEFORE the script is handed to the human (before journey-handoff): run this check against the unit's state file; a non-zero exit means the hand-off is BLOCKED — keep grinding the agent track (or record an explicit deferred-as-risk in deferred_risk[]), never push a harness-assertable step onto the human. The check is a pure function of the per-criterion harness_drivable/track/status/evidence fields (state-schema.md), never prose the writer is asked to honor: every harness-drivable criterion must be on the agent track and agent-verified (with non-empty real-system evidence) or deferred, and every criterion on the human track must be flagged not-harness-drivable.

A STALLED MACHINE-CHECKABLE CRITERION IS A BLOCKER THE AGENT OWNS, NEVER A HUMAN DECISION FORK (AC-4, AC-9). When a criterion the project's own harness can drive to a real-system assertion (an AGENT-track / harness_drivable criterion) will not pass, the agent has EXACTLY TWO exits, both inside its own lane: (a) fix the obstacle and re-run the real-system harness, or (b) record an explicit deferred_risk[] edge in the human's OWN words. The configured e2e harness on the launch machine is the SOLE proof venue by definition, so there is NO venue/host question to put to the human — asking 'where should I prove this, here or somewhere else?' or otherwise handing a stalled machine proof to the human as a decision is FORBIDDEN, the generic stop-and-ask reflex misfiring. This is mechanically gated, not remembered: BEFORE sending any human-facing STOP turn, run this check against the unit's state file. A non-zero exit means an agent-track (harness_drivable) criterion is still pending with NO deferred_risk edge naming it — the STOP is REJECTED; keep grinding the harness or record the deferred_risk, do NOT escalate. The check is a pure function of the per-criterion harness_drivable/status fields and the deferred_risk[] edges (state-schema.md): every harness_drivable criterion still pending must be covered by a deferred_risk edge (linked by its criterion id, or an agent-track edge naming the id), never prose the writer is asked to honor.
